summary: Fix cargo test's Windows compile break â€” consolidate the triplicated SDK-vendoring test helper into tests/common, using a junction (not a Unix symlink) for the npm-install stand-in

new.description: New shared test-support module hoisting the byte-identical `sdk_root()`/`ensure_sdk_built()` today triplicated verbatim (tests/emit.rs:628-641, tests/install.rs:57-70, tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs:37-50), plus a new `vendor_sdk(node_modules_scope: &Path)`: ensures the `@dtmd` scope dir exists, is idempotent (skip if the link/junction already exists â€” install.rs's existing guard, generalized), calls `ensure_sdk_built()`, then links `sdk_root()` into it as the stand-in for a real `npm install`'s local-dependency resolution. `#[cfg(unix)]` keeps `std::os::unix::fs::symlink(target, link)`; `#[cfg(windows)]` shells `cmd /C mklink /J <link> <target>` â€” note mklink's arg order is Link-then-Target, reversed vs symlink's (original, link), and quote paths since CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR may contain spaces. A junction (never `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir`) needs no SeCreateSymbolicLinkPrivilege/Developer Mode, matching how npm itself links local/workspace deps on Windows â€” cite npm/cli#5189, nixhacker.com 'Understanding and Exploiting Symbolic links in Windows', hinchley.net 'Junctions and Symbolic Links', all retrieved 2026-07-08, into the code comment (rust.md comment bar).

edit.description: Add `mod common;`; delete the local `sdk_root`/`ensure_sdk_built` (lines 628-641); in `wire_sdk_harness_program` (line 699) replace the unconditional `std::os::unix::fs::symlink(sdk_root(), node_modules_scope.join("temper"))` with `common::vendor_sdk(&node_modules_scope)`.

Add `mod common;`; delete the local `sdk_root`/`ensure_sdk_built` (lines 57-70) and the local `vendor_sdk` (lines 76-86, unconditional symlink at line 84) â€” its one caller uses `common::vendor_sdk` instead.

Add `mod common;`; delete the local `sdk_root`/`ensure_sdk_built` (lines 37-50); in `wire_memberless_harness` (line 105) replace the unconditional `std::os::unix::fs::symlink(...)` with `common::vendor_sdk(&node_modules_scope)`.

tests.asserts: the real-SDK harness tests (e.g. `wire_sdk_harness`) still pass unchanged on unix after the extraction â€” same symlink behavior via the shared helper
install's real-SDK-vendored tests still pass unchanged after `vendor_sdk` moves to tests/common
the frozen-lock byte-compare test against the memberless harness still passes after `wire_memberless_harness` uses the shared helper

acceptance: `cargo test` compiles and the full suite runs to completion on Windows â€” no unconditional unix-only symlink call remains in tests/; on unix, `vendor_sdk`'s behavior and the full suite (229/229 per the field report) are unchanged.
notes: Windows field report observed at baac085; junction-not-symlink_dir fix verified 2026-07-08 (nixhacker.com, hinchley.net, npm/cli#5189 â€” carry cites into the code comment, rust.md). No new crate: shell `cmd /C mklink /J <link> <target>` (arg order reversed vs std::os::unix::fs::symlink's (original, link)). CI is ubuntu-only â€” build can't execute the Windows path itself; say so in the commit body. scoped at 16f844b

summary: Consolidate the two independent glob engines (kind.rs's hand-rolled segment matcher, graph.rs's hand-rolled regex compiler) onto the sanctioned globset crate

edit.description: Add `globset` as a direct dependency â€” already sanctioned (CLAUDE.md Tech stack: 'already inside `ignore`; never hand-roll matching') and already in the dependency tree transitively via `ignore = "0.4"`, so this pins the version rather than widening the sanctioned set.

Delete the hand-rolled `pub(crate) fn glob_matches(glob: &str, name: &str) -> bool` (lines 429-456, single-star backtracking, one path segment only). Replace its one in-module caller (line 357, `self.governs.glob_leaf()` against a filename) and its two cross-module callers (src/import.rs:230, src/coverage_note.rs:262, both matching one path segment/filename) with a `globset::Glob` compiled from the same leaf pattern â€” globset matches a bare segment (no `/`) as today's matcher does, so this is a like-for-like swap, not a semantics change.

Delete `glob_matches_any` (line 630), `glob_to_regex` (line 641, hand-rolled `**`/`*`/`?` to anchored-regex compiler), and `is_regex_meta` (its escaping helper) â€” replace the one caller with a `globset::GlobBuilder` compiled once per glob and tested against each candidate path, preserving the same semantics: `**` crosses `/` (including a bare leading `**/` matching zero segments), `*`/`?` stay within one segment, a glob globset cannot compile still degrades to 'matches' (never a false negative on an unparseable pattern â€” the existing defensive fallback, preserved as a `.ok()`-style guard around the compile).

`collect_glob`'s per-segment walk (lines ~195-235) currently calls `crate::kind::glob_matches(segment, name)` per directory-tree level â€” this still needs to walk the tree level by level (globset does not enumerate a filesystem), but the per-segment test at line 230 switches to the shared `globset`-backed matcher from kind.rs instead of the hand-rolled one.
`governs` (line 255) calls `glob_matches(kind.governs.glob_leaf(), leaf)` on a single filename â€” switch to the shared `globset`-backed matcher.

tests.asserts: `glob_to_regex_matches_common_path_globs_within_and_across_segments` (line 1576) and every `**`/`*`/`?` case it covers still pass against the globset-backed matcher
custom-kind `governs` glob-leaf matching over on-disk fixtures is unchanged
any end-to-end discovery/check run touching a `**`-governed custom kind still finds the same members

acceptance: One glob-matching surface backed by `globset`; `kind::glob_matches`, `graph::glob_to_regex`, `graph::glob_matches_any`, and `graph::is_regex_meta` no longer exist; every caller (kind.rs, import.rs, coverage_note.rs, graph.rs) resolves through it; `cargo test` green.
notes: Refactor capture session-two-glob-engines.md (observed at 0ccba8d), re-verified live at HEAD â€” both engines and all four caller sites confirmed present, unchanged since. kind.rs's own comment admits the hand-roll was 'short of pulling in a glob crate for one metacharacter' â€” globset is now the sanctioned answer. import.rs's directory walk stays (globset can't enumerate a filesystem); only its per-segment test call changes. scoped at 390e085

summary: Consolidate the two lexical path normalizers (import.rs drops only `.`, graph.rs's is the strict `.`+`..`-collapsing superset) into one home

edit.description: Make the existing `fn normalize_path(path: &Path) -> PathBuf` (line 812 â€” drops `Component::CurDir`, collapses `Component::ParentDir` against a preceding `Normal` segment, keeps a leading `..` with nothing to pop) `pub(crate)` â€” it is the strict superset and stays the one home.

Delete the local `fn normalize(path: &Path) -> PathBuf` (lines 283-287, drops only `Component::CurDir`). Its three callers (lines 214, 224, 274, all comparing a walk entry's path against the `discoverable` set built the same way) switch to `crate::graph::normalize_path` â€” confirm no caller's path ever carries a `..` segment that the narrower normalizer was relying on NOT collapsing (a walk entry under a fixed root should not), and note this in the commit body if any behavior-relevant case is found.

tests.asserts: path-normalization-dependent resolution (`normalize_path` call sites at graph.rs:758,766,804) is unchanged
discovery over a harness tree (import.rs's `discoverable`/`collect_glob` machinery) still finds the same members after switching normalizers

acceptance: One `normalize_path` (graph.rs, `pub(crate)`); `import::normalize` no longer exists; every caller uses the shared one; `cargo test` green.
notes: Refactor capture session-path-normalizers.md (observed at 0ccba8d), re-verified live at HEAD â€” both functions confirmed present, unchanged since; kept coverage_note.rs's `normalize_root` and drift.rs's `normalize_lf` untouched per the capture (different jobs, name collision only). Serialized behind GLOB-ENGINE-CONSOLIDATE â€” both entries edit src/import.rs and src/graph.rs; two open entries on the same files conflict at merge. scoped at 390e085

summary: Consolidate the byte-identical `plural(n)` suffix helper (and two inline duplicate closures in install.rs) into one home

edit.description: Add `pub(crate) fn plural(n: usize) -> &'static str { if n == 1 { "" } else { "s" } }` â€” homed here as the module already devoted to rendering member/report text (its own docs: 'the nested-member display rule'), a fair shared home for a tiny cross-cutting text-rendering helper neither bundle.rs nor coverage_note.rs owns more legitimately than the other.
Delete the local `fn plural(count: usize) -> &'static str` (line 324); its two call sites in `render` (lines ~308-312) use `crate::display::plural`.
Delete the local `fn plural(n: usize) -> &'static str` (line 221); its two call sites (lines 124, 126) use `crate::display::plural`.

Two inline byte-identical closures â€” `let plural = if modelines == 1 { "" } else { "s" };` (line ~494) and the same shape at line ~499 for `notes` â€” found while re-verifying this capture against HEAD, same job as the two named duplicates. Replace both with `crate::display::plural(modelines)` / `crate::display::plural(notes)`.

tests.asserts: the bundle report's terminal rendering (`wrote <path>` lines plus the tally line) is byte-identical after the helper moves
coverage-note prose rendering with 1 vs N members/kinds is unchanged
install's discovery-report rendering (modeline/managed-by-note counts) is unchanged for 1 vs N counts

acceptance: One `plural` helper (`src/display.rs`, `pub(crate)`); no duplicate definition or inline closure remains in bundle.rs, coverage_note.rs, or install.rs; `cargo test` green.
notes: Refactor capture session-plural-helper.md (observed at 0ccba8d) named bundle.rs+coverage_note.rs only; install.rs's two inline closures are the same job, found re-verifying at HEAD â€” consolidation is the sanctioned expansion (engineering.md), named here rather than left as fresh residue. Serialized behind GLOB-ENGINE-CONSOLIDATE purely to keep the src/ consolidation wave ordered one-open-at-a-time; no direct file overlap between the two. scoped at 390e085

summary: Consolidate the two lock-row predicate decoders (builtin.rs's member-clause set, main.rs's requirement-clause set) beside the Predicate enum, one match arm set for the whole vocabulary

edit.description: Add a `pub(crate) fn predicate_from_row(row: &drift::ClauseRow) -> Option<Predicate>` covering the FULL `Predicate` enum's row decoding â€” today split across `src/builtin.rs`'s `predicate_from_row` (required/optional/min_len/max_len/max_lines/allowed_chars/forbidden_keys/deny/name-matches-dir/unique-name) and `src/main.rs`'s inline match in `clause_from_row` (count/unique/membership/degree). Homed beside `enum Predicate` (line 80) â€” the vocabulary authority the capture names. Decodes ONLY the predicate â€” not severity/guidance/cite â€” so each call site's existing strict-vs-tolerant severity handling and guidance/cite handling are preserved as thin call-site logic, not folded in (see notes: main.rs today silently drops guidance/cite for requirement clauses while builtin.rs keeps them â€” this entry preserves that existing behavior verbatim; deciding whether that drop is a bug is not this entry's job).

Delete `predicate_from_row` (lines 38-71); `clause_from_row` (line 80) calls `contract::predicate_from_row` instead, keeping its own `.expect(...)` strict-severity behavior and its guidance/cite population unchanged.

`clause_from_row` (line 1082) replaces its inline match with a call to `contract::predicate_from_row`, keeping its own tolerant `?` severity handling and `guidance: None, source: None` unchanged (see contract.rs entry note on preserving existing behavior).

tests.asserts: member-clause (required/min_len/allowed_chars/etc.) decoding off the built-in lock is unchanged
round-trip of clause rows through the shared decoder is unchanged
requirement-clause (count/unique/membership/degree) decoding off a committed lock is unchanged

acceptance: One predicate-decoding match (`contract::predicate_from_row`) covering the full `Predicate` enum; `src/builtin.rs` and `src/main.rs` each delegate to it, keeping only their own severity/guidance/cite assembly; `cargo test` green.
notes: Capture session-clause-from-row-twins.md (observed 0ccba8d), re-verified live at HEAD. Close read found the two decoders cover DISJOINT predicate subsets (member vs requirement scope), not overlapping duplicates as the capture implied â€” still two hand-maintained match arms over one closed enum, the 'two check paths' risk engineering.md names. main.rs's guidance/cite drop looks possibly unintentional; fixing it is out of scope â€” flag in commit body. scoped at 390e085

summary: Extract the SDK's version-stamped seam-encoding convention (hand-inlined twice) into one encodeSeam helper

edit.description: Add `export function encodeSeam(payload: object): string { return JSON.stringify({ version: SEAM_VERSION, ...payload }, null, 2) + "\n"; }` beside `SEAM_VERSION` (line 369). Rewrite `declarationsToJson` (line 377) to `return encodeSeam(declarations);` â€” same payload shape (`{version, ...declarations}`), byte-identical output.

In `emit`'s `compile` closure (line ~293), replace `JSON.stringify({ version: SEAM_VERSION, declarations, members }, null, 2) + "\n"` with `encodeSeam({ declarations, members })`, importing `encodeSeam` alongside the existing `SEAM_VERSION, compileDeclarations` import (line 20). Same payload shape, byte-identical output â€” the double-emit byte-compare (`first.seam !== second.seam`) is the existing determinism guard and stays unchanged.

tests.asserts: line 259's `JSON.parse(declarationsToJson(compileDeclarations(fullHarness())))` still round-trips identically through the shared helper

acceptance: One `encodeSeam(payload)` helper in `sdk/src/declarations.ts`; both `declarationsToJson` and `emit`'s seam construction call it; `pnpm --dir sdk test` green (strict tsc + node --test).
notes: Refactor capture session-seam-encode-twin.md (observed at 0ccba8d), re-verified live at HEAD â€” both inline encodings confirmed present, unchanged since, same version-stamp+trailing-newline convention over different payload shapes (declarations-only vs declarations+members), which `encodeSeam(payload: object)` accommodates without loss. scoped at 390e085

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels â€” SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle â€” fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist â€” a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, now at 0.0.5 pending publish â€” SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published â€” the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
